Party_Details.Name: Ng Tang Hock

Facts.Text: Ng Tang Hock is a successful self-made businessman.
Teelek Realty was incorporated in Singapore.
Ng Tang Hock transferred his shares in Mingsen to Wendy.
Loans from Ng Tang Hock to Teelek Realty totaled $12,564,000.
Ng Tang Hock met with Shirlyn and Eugene at West Coast Plaza.
Interim judgment dissolving the marriage was granted.
A consent ancillaries order was made in the divorce proceedings.
Ng Tang Hock resigned as a director of Teelek Realty.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the plaintiff is entitled to repayment of loans made to Teelek Realty and whether the conduct of the defendants amounted to oppression.
Party_Details.Rule: A shareholder may seek relief under s 216 of the Companies Act if the company's affairs are conducted in a manner oppressive to the shareholder.
Party_Details.Application: The plaintiff argued that the loans were not time-barred and that the defendants' actions were oppressive. The defendants claimed the loans were waived and denied oppressive conduct.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the plaintiff's oppression claim was valid and ordered the winding up of Teelek Realty. The claim for repayment of loans was dismissed.

Party_Details.Name: Teelek Realty Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Teelek Realty was incorporated as an investment holding company.
Loans from Ng Tang Hock were reclassified as loans from Wendy.
Shirlyn was appointed as a director of Teelek Realty.
Eugene was appointed as a director of Teelek Realty.
Wendy transferred one share each to Shirlyn and Eugene.
Notice of Teelek Realty’s 20th AGM was issued.
Ng Tang Hock commenced the present action.
The 20th AGM was held based on the original agenda.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendants' actions in managing Teelek Realty were oppressive to the plaintiff as a shareholder.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act in the best interests of the company and not disregard the interests of shareholders.
Party_Details.Application: The defendants argued that their actions were in compliance with company procedures and that the plaintiff's claims were unfounded. The plaintiff contended that the defendants' actions were prejudicial.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the defendants' conduct amounted to oppression and ordered the winding up of Teelek Realty.

Party_Details.Name: Wendy

Facts.Text: Wendy married Ng Tang Hock.
Wendy became a director of Teelek Realty.
Wendy received shares in Mingsen from Ng Tang Hock.
Wendy reclassified loans from Ng Tang Hock as loans from herself.
Wendy was granted joint custody of children in the Ancillaries Order.
Wendy transferred shares to Shirlyn and Eugene.
Wendy's lawyer sent a draft Trust Deed to Ng Tang Hock.
Wendy filed a counterclaim against Ng Tang Hock.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Wendy is entitled to claim that Ng Tang Hock holds shares in Teelek Realty on trust for Shirlyn and Eugene.
Party_Details.Rule: A trust claim requires clear evidence of an agreement to hold property on behalf of beneficiaries.
Party_Details.Application: Wendy claimed that there was an agreement for Ng Tang Hock to hold shares on trust. Ng Tang Hock denied any such agreement existed.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of a trust agreement and dismissed Wendy's counterclaim.

Party_Details.Name: Ng Tang Hock

Facts.Text: Ng Tang Hock is a successful self-made businessman.
Ng Tang Hock married Wendy.
Ng Tang Hock became a director of Teelek Realty.
Ng Tang Hock transferred shares in Mingsen to Wendy.
Ng Tang Hock met with Shirlyn and Eugene at West Coast Plaza.
Interim judgment dissolving the marriage was granted.
A consent ancillaries order was made in the divorce proceedings.
Ng Tang Hock resigned as a director of Teelek Realty.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Ng Tang Hock holds shares in Teelek Realty on trust for Shirlyn and Eugene.
Party_Details.Rule: A trust requires a clear intention to create a management relationship and hold property for the benefit of others.
Party_Details.Application: Ng Tang Hock denied any trust agreement and argued that he retained full ownership of his shares. Wendy claimed there was an agreement for a trust.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence supporting the existence of a trust and ruled in favor of Ng Tang Hock.
